categories.name: Election Interference
categories.description: Interference in free, fair, and accessible elections; dismantled safeguards and security; disenfranchisement; circumvention of constitutionally prescribed presidential term limits

rubric.1-2: Normal democratic activity, minor procedural disputes
rubric.3-4: Elevated litigation, some restrictive laws passed, redistricting concerns
rubric.5-6: Significant restrictions enacted, credible interference allegations, election security dismantled
rubric.7-8: Systematic disenfranchisement efforts, federal intervention in voting procedures, defiance of election outcomes
rubric.9-10: Overt attempts to overturn results, term limit violations, election infrastructure compromised

categories.name: Legal / Defying Court Orders
categories.description: Ignoring rulings from the Supreme Court or lower courts; retributive prosecutions; undermining access to legal representation; due process violations

rubric.1-2: Normal legal disputes, appeals through proper channels
rubric.3-4: Aggressive legal postures, delayed compliance with court orders
rubric.5-6: Pattern of non-compliance, prosecutorial discretion concerns, retributive investigations
rubric.7-8: Open defiance of court orders, clear retributive prosecution pattern, judges targeted
rubric.9-10: Systematic rule of law breakdown, court orders routinely ignored, rule by decree

categories.name: National Security
categories.description: Domestic deployment of active-duty military or National Guard for political purposes; dismantling cyber defenses; politicization of intelligence community; unwarranted military action abroad

rubric.1-2: Apolitical security services, normal oversight functioning, routine deployments
rubric.3-4: Unusual rhetoric, minor organizational changes, increased political pressure on agencies
rubric.5-6: Significant cyber defense reductions, politicized appointments, intelligence sharing restricted
rubric.7-8: Domestic military use for political purposes, major security gaps, politically motivated strikes
rubric.9-10: Military deployed against civilians, critical defenses dismantled, intelligence used for suppression

categories.name: Intimidation & Political Violence
categories.description: Encouraging or providing cover for intimidation or political violence against judges, political opponents, journalists, or others who hold a different opinion; pardoning political violence

rubric.1-2: Baseline threat levels, isolated incidents, healthy political debate
rubric.3-4: Elevated threats, concerning rhetoric from officials, increasing polarization
rubric.5-6: Pattern of intimidation, sporadic political violence, dehumanizing rhetoric normalized
rubric.7-8: Systematic targeting of judges and opponents, mass pardons for political violence, armed groups mobilizing
rubric.9-10: State-sanctioned violence, journalists imprisoned, widespread political violence

categories.name: Business Interference
categories.description: Government taking stakes in or control over private companies; pressuring executive firings; imposing loyalty tests on business leaders; weaponizing regulatory agencies against specific firms

rubric.3-4: Increased regulatory uncertainty, public pressure on specific companies
rubric.5-6: Government pressuring executive decisions, regulatory agencies used selectively
rubric.7-8: Government demanding loyalty from business leaders, forced investments or policy compliance, executive firings pressured
rubric.9-10: Government directing business operations, systematic coercion of private sector

categories.name: Major Economic Disruptions
categories.description: Government actions that severely damage the economy or markets; major, disruptive trade tariffs; destabilizing the dollar; neglecting critical policy action; supply chain disruption

rubric.1-2: Stable trade relationships, predictable economic policy
rubric.3-4: Trade tensions, renegotiation of agreements, some tariff escalation
rubric.5-6: Significant tariff actions, supply chain disruptions, retaliatory spirals beginning
rubric.7-8: Trade wars, GDP-impacting tariff levels, economic isolation policies
rubric.9-10: Trade breakdown, severe supply chain collapse, complete withdrawal from global system

categories.name: Cronyism & Retaliation
categories.description: Undermining market competition by awarding contracts or regulatory advantages to politically favored companies; punishing businesses through public threats, investigations, IRS audits, antitrust actions, contract cancellations, or executive orders

rubric.1-2: Transparent procurement, merit-based contracting, normal regulatory activity
rubric.3-4: Some questionable awards, elevated scrutiny of critics, concerns about political favoritism
rubric.5-6: Pattern of favoritism, documented retaliation cases, blacklisting critics
rubric.7-8: Systematic cronyism, weaponized regulation, contracts contingent on political support
rubric.9-10: Overt pay-to-play, businesses destroyed for political reasons, state resources distributed to loyalists

categories.name: Fiscal & Monetary Policy
categories.description: Federal Reserve independence, debt sustainability, fiscal responsibility; interfering with independent reporting of economic data; government shutdown risks

rubric.1-2: Independent central bank, sustainable fiscal trajectory
rubric.3-4: Political pressure on Fed, elevated but manageable deficits
rubric.5-6: Credible threats to Fed independence, unsustainable fiscal path, data integrity concerns
rubric.7-8: Fed independence compromised, debt crisis emerging, economic data manipulated
rubric.9-10: Political control of monetary policy, fiscal collapse, data fabrication

categories.name: Public Pressure & Polarization
categories.description: Demands on companies to take political stances; consumer boycott campaigns driven by political actors; pressure on businesses to align with administration priorities or face public retaliation

rubric.1-2: Normal public discourse about corporate responsibility, market-driven consumer choices
rubric.3-4: Increased political scrutiny of corporate positions, occasional boycott threats
rubric.5-6: Organized pressure campaigns targeting companies, executives publicly called out for political views
rubric.7-8: Systematic political demands on businesses, companies forced to choose sides, reputational attacks on non-compliant firms
rubric.9-10: Businesses unable to operate apolitically, political alignment required for market access

categories.name: Suppression of Freedom of Expression
categories.description: Interference of press freedom, from jailing journalists to threatening or fining media outlets; punishing free speech or coercing speech to promote administration priorities; interfering with First Amendment rights of universities via funding freezes

rubric.1-2: Robust press freedom, healthy media ecosystem, normal academic debates
rubric.3-4: Increased hostility toward press, some access restrictions, concerning rhetoric
rubric.5-6: Pattern of retaliation, university funding cuts with political motive, delegitimization of media
rubric.7-8: Systematic suppression, self-censorship widespread, legal harassment of journalists
rubric.9-10: Press effectively controlled, universities silenced, journalists imprisoned or deported

categories.name: Erosion of Institutions & Norms
categories.description: Gutting federal agencies; undermining watchdogs and inspectors general; disrupting data transparency; mass firings of career officials; dismantling oversight capacity

rubric.1-2: Independent civil service, robust oversight mechanisms, routine reorganization
rubric.3-4: Elevated departures, some data access concerns, political pressure on civil servants
rubric.5-6: Significant workforce reductions, IG independence compromised, loyalty tests for officials
rubric.7-8: Systematic hollowing out, data suppression, mass purges of career officials
rubric.9-10: Agencies non-functional, transparency destroyed, complete politicization of government
